---
title: "TD_P003: Component-wise Testing Principle"
subtitle: "Test interactive applications at the component level for better isolation and debugging"
chapter: "CH05"
category: "principle"
number: "TD_P003"
date-created: "2025-04-07"
date-modified: "2025-08-17"
author: "Claude"
type: "principle"
law: "Testing & Deployment Law"
article: "Article 3"
derives_from:
  - "DEV_P001: Debug Principles"
  - "DEV_P005: Debug Efficiency Exception"
influences:
  - "UI_R001: UI-Server-Defaults N-tuple"
  - "SO_R021: One Function One File"
related_to:
  - "TD_P002: Dual Testing Approach"
  - "TD_R001: Shiny Test Data"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article Overview {#overview}

**Testing & Deployment Law Article 3 (Component-wise Testing Principle)**

This article establishes that interactive applications, especially those with complex UIs like Shiny apps, must be tested at the component level rather than only as complete systems, enabling isolation of failures, more efficient debugging, and better handling of edge cases.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Principle
Each component must be independently testable with its own test harness, test data generators, and comprehensive test scenarios covering normal operation, edge cases, and error conditions.
:::

# Section 1: Rationale {#rationale}

## Subsection 1: Why Component Testing {#why}

Shiny applications and similar interactive systems can easily collapse under complexity, making it difficult to diagnose issues when testing only the complete system. Component-wise testing provides:

1. **Isolation of Issues**: Separates UI/UX problems from data and logic issues
2. **Independent Development**: Allows components to be developed and debugged separately
3. **Systematic Edge Case Testing**: Enables thorough testing of edge cases per component
4. **Refactoring Safety**: Ensures components remain functional after changes
5. **Incremental Integration**: Facilitates step-by-step system assembly

## Subsection 2: Benefits {#benefits}

第一項：開發效率提升 (Development efficiency improvement)

- Faster development cycles through isolated testing
- Parallel development of components by different team members
- Reduced time to identify and fix issues
- Better code reusability across projects

第二項：品質保證 (Quality assurance)

- More comprehensive test coverage
- Better handling of edge cases
- Improved error isolation and diagnosis
- Higher confidence in component reliability

第三項：維護性改善 (Maintainability improvement)

- Test scripts serve as usage documentation
- Components can be tested against future requirements
- Easier onboarding of new developers
- Clear component interfaces and contracts

# Section 2: Component Isolation Strategy {#isolation}

## Subsection 1: Sandboxed Testing Environment {#sandbox}

第一項：最小測試應用程式 (Minimal test applications)

```r
# Create minimal test app for each component
library(shiny)
library(bs4Dash)

# Test app for CustomerProfileModule
ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("CustomerProfile Component Test"),
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      h4("Test Controls"),
      selectInput("scenario", "Test Scenario",
                  choices = c("Normal", "Empty", "Invalid", "Extreme")),
      actionButton("refresh", "Refresh Data"),
      hr(),
      h4("Debug Options"),
      checkboxInput("show_debug", "Show Debug Info", TRUE)
    ),
    mainPanel(
      # Component under test
      customerProfileUI("test"),
      conditionalPanel(
        condition = "input.show_debug",
        verbatimTextOutput("debug_output")
      )
    )
  )
)

server <- function(input, output, session) {
  # Test data generation
  test_data <- reactive({
    input$refresh  # Trigger on refresh
    create_test_data(tolower(input$scenario))
  })
  
  # Initialize component
  result <- customerProfileServer("test", data = test_data)
  
  # Debug output
  output$debug_output <- renderPrint({
    list(
      scenario = input$scenario,
      data_rows = nrow(test_data()),
      data_structure = str(test_data()),
      component_output = result()
    )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
```

第二項：合成資料產生器 (Synthetic data generators)

```r
# Test data generator function
create_test_data <- function(scenario = "normal") {
  set.seed(123)  # Reproducible test data
  
  switch(scenario,
    "normal" = data.frame(
      customer_id = sprintf("CUST%03d", 1:100),
      name = paste("Customer", 1:100),
      value = rnorm(100, 1000, 200),
      status = sample(c("Active", "Inactive"), 100, replace = TRUE),
      last_purchase = Sys.Date() - sample(1:365, 100, replace = TRUE)
    ),
    
    "empty" = data.frame(
      customer_id = character(0),
      name = character(0),
      value = numeric(0),
      status = character(0),
      last_purchase = as.Date(character(0))
    ),
    
    "invalid" = data.frame(
      customer_id = c(NA, "", "INVALID", "CUST001"),
      name = c("Test", NA, "", "Valid Customer"),
      value = c(NA, -1000, Inf, 500),
      status = c("Unknown", NA, "", "Active"),
      last_purchase = c(NA, NA, NA, Sys.Date())
    ),
    
    "extreme" = data.frame(
      customer_id = sprintf("CUST%09d", 1:10000),
      name = strrep("LongCustomerName", 50),
      value = c(rep(1e9, 5000), rep(-1e9, 5000)),
      status = rep("Active", 10000),
      last_purchase = rep(as.Date("1900-01-01"), 10000)
    )
  )
}
```

## Subsection 2: Independent Component Files {#independent}

第一項：無依賴設計 (Dependency-free design)

```r
# Component designed to be testable without dependencies
customerProfileUI <- function(id, defaults = customerProfileDefaults()) {
  ns <- NS(id)
  
  tagList(
    box(
      title = "Customer Profile",
      status = "primary",
      solidHeader = TRUE,
      width = 12,
      
      # UI elements with defaults
      valueBoxOutput(ns("total_value")),
      plotOutput(ns("history_plot")),
      DTOutput(ns("details_table"))
    )
  )
}

customerProfileServer <- function(id, data = reactive(NULL), 
                                   config = reactive(NULL)) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    # Handle missing data gracefully
    safe_data <- reactive({
      d <- data()
      if (is.null(d) || nrow(d) == 0) {
        return(create_default_data())
      }
      validate_and_clean(d)
    })
    
    # Component logic...
  })
}

customerProfileDefaults <- function() {
  list(
    empty_message = "No customer data available",
    default_plot_type = "line",
    max_rows_display = 100,
    value_format = "currency"
  )
}
```

## Subsection 3: Test Matrix {#test-matrix}

第一項：測試矩陣定義 (Test matrix definition)

```r
# Define comprehensive test matrix
component_test_matrix <- tribble(
  ~test_id, ~scenario, ~data_condition, ~expected_result, ~priority,
  "T001", "Normal operation", "valid_100_rows", "All visualizations render", "High",
  "T002", "Empty data", "zero_rows", "Shows empty state message", "High",
  "T003", "NULL data", "null_input", "Falls back to defaults", "High",
  "T004", "Missing columns", "partial_schema", "Handles gracefully", "Medium",
  "T005", "NA values", "contains_na", "Skips NA in calculations", "High",
  "T006", "Single row", "one_row", "Renders without error", "Medium",
  "T007", "Large dataset", "10000_rows", "Performance acceptable", "Low",
  "T008", "Special characters", "unicode_data", "Displays correctly", "Low",
  "T009", "Negative values", "negative_numbers", "Handles appropriately", "Medium",
  "T010", "Date boundaries", "extreme_dates", "No overflow errors", "Low"
)

# Execute test matrix
run_component_tests <- function(component_name, test_matrix) {
  results <- list()
  
  for (i in seq_len(nrow(test_matrix))) {
    test <- test_matrix[i, ]
    
    result <- tryCatch({
      # Generate test data
      test_data <- generate_test_scenario(test$data_condition)
      
      # Run component test
      test_component(component_name, test_data)
      
      list(
        test_id = test$test_id,
        status = "PASS",
        message = "Test completed successfully"
      )
    }, error = function(e) {
      list(
        test_id = test$test_id,
        status = "FAIL",
        message = e$message
      )
    })
    
    results[[i]] <- result
  }
  
  # Return test report
  bind_rows(results)
}
```

# Section 3: Required Testing Artifacts {#artifacts}

## Subsection 1: Component Test File {#test-file}

第一項：獨立測試腳本 (Standalone test script)

```r
# File: test_customerProfile.R
#' @title Component Test for CustomerProfile Module
#' @principle TD_P003 Component-wise Testing
#' @description Comprehensive test suite for CustomerProfile component

# Setup ----
library(shiny)
library(testthat)
source("customerProfile.R")

# Test Data Generators ----
source("test_data_generators.R")

# Test Scenarios ----
test_that("CustomerProfile handles normal data", {
  data <- create_test_data("normal")
  
  testServer(customerProfileServer, args = list(data = reactive(data)), {
    expect_true(!is.null(output$total_value))
    expect_true(!is.null(output$history_plot))
    expect_equal(nrow(output$details_table), 100)
  })
})

test_that("CustomerProfile handles empty data", {
  data <- create_test_data("empty")
  
  testServer(customerProfileServer, args = list(data = reactive(data)), {
    expect_equal(output$total_value$value, 0)
    expect_true(grepl("No data", output$empty_message))
  })
})

test_that("CustomerProfile handles invalid data", {
  data <- create_test_data("invalid")
  
  testServer(customerProfileServer, args = list(data = reactive(data)), {
    # Should not error out
    expect_error(output$total_value, NA)
    # Should filter invalid rows
    expect_true(output$row_count < nrow(data))
  })
})

# Visual Test App ----
if (interactive()) {
  run_visual_test <- function() {
    shinyApp(
      ui = customerProfileTestUI(),
      server = customerProfileTestServer()
    )
  }
  
  run_visual_test()
}
```

## Subsection 2: Test Data Generators {#generators}

第一項：場景特定資料產生 (Scenario-specific data generation)

```r
# File: test_data_generators.R
#' Generate test data for various scenarios

# Edge case generators
generate_edge_cases <- function() {
  list(
    empty_strings = data.frame(
      id = c("", "  ", "\t", "\n"),
      value = c(100, 200, 300, 400)
    ),
    
    special_chars = data.frame(
      id = c("O'Brien", "José", "北京", "👍"),
      value = c(100, 200, 300, 400)
    ),
    
    boundary_values = data.frame(
      id = 1:6,
      value = c(0, 1, -1, .Machine$double.xmax, 
                .Machine$double.xmin, .Machine$double.eps)
    ),
    
    missing_patterns = data.frame(
      id = 1:4,
      value = c(NA, NaN, Inf, -Inf)
    )
  )
}

# Performance test data
generate_performance_data <- function(n = 10000) {
  data.frame(
    id = seq_len(n),
    category = sample(LETTERS, n, replace = TRUE),
    value = rnorm(n, 1000, 200),
    timestamp = Sys.time() - runif(n, 0, 365*24*60*60),
    text = replicate(n, paste(sample(letters, 50, replace = TRUE), 
                               collapse = ""))
  )
}

# Time series test data
generate_time_series_data <- function(periods = 365) {
  dates <- seq(Sys.Date() - periods, Sys.Date(), by = "day")
  
  data.frame(
    date = dates,
    value = cumsum(rnorm(length(dates))),
    events = sample(c(NA, "Event"), length(dates), 
                    replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.95, 0.05))
  )
}
```

## Subsection 3: Component Documentation {#documentation}

第一項：預期行為文檔 (Expected behavior documentation)

```r
# File: customerProfile_test_spec.md
#' # CustomerProfile Component Test Specification
#' 
#' ## Expected Behaviors
#' 
#' ### Normal Operation
#' - Displays customer name and ID
#' - Shows total value as formatted currency
#' - Renders purchase history plot
#' - Displays details in sortable table
#' 
#' ### Empty Data
#' - Shows "No customer data available" message
#' - Value boxes display zero with gray background
#' - Plot area shows instructional message
#' - Table shows empty state
#' 
#' ### Error Conditions
#' - Invalid customer ID: Shows "Unknown Customer"
#' - Missing required fields: Uses defaults
#' - Calculation errors: Shows "--" in value boxes
#' - Plot errors: Shows error message in plot area
#' 
#' ## Screenshots
#' - Normal state: ./screenshots/normal.png
#' - Empty state: ./screenshots/empty.png
#' - Error state: ./screenshots/error.png
#' 
#' ## Common Issues
#' 1. Slow rendering with >1000 rows
#'    - Solution: Implement pagination
#' 2. Memory issues with reactive data
#'    - Solution: Use reactiveVal with cleanup
```

# Section 4: Testing Methods by Component Type {#methods}

## Subsection 1: UI Components {#ui-testing}

第一項：UI 測試重點 (UI testing focus)

```r
# Test UI rendering and responsiveness
test_ui_component <- function(ui_function, test_id = "test") {
  # Test rendering across screen sizes
  screen_sizes <- list(
    mobile = c(375, 667),
    tablet = c(768, 1024),
    desktop = c(1920, 1080)
  )
  
  for (size_name in names(screen_sizes)) {
    size <- screen_sizes[[size_name]]
    
    # Create test app with specific viewport
    test_app <- shinyApp(
      ui = fluidPage(
        tags$head(
          tags$meta(
            name = "viewport",
            content = sprintf("width=%d, height=%d", size[1], size[2])
          )
        ),
        ui_function(test_id)
      ),
      server = function(input, output, session) {}
    )
    
    # Verify rendering (would use snapshot testing in practice)
    expect_error(test_app, NA)
  }
}

# Test accessibility
test_accessibility <- function(ui_element) {
  # Check for ARIA labels
  expect_true(
    grepl("aria-label|role=", as.character(ui_element))
  )
  
  # Check for keyboard navigation
  expect_true(
    grepl("tabindex", as.character(ui_element))
  )
}
```

## Subsection 2: Server Components {#server-testing}

第一項：伺服器邏輯測試 (Server logic testing)

```r
# Test server reactivity and calculations
test_server_component <- function(server_function, test_scenarios) {
  for (scenario in test_scenarios) {
    testServer(server_function, 
               args = list(data = reactive(scenario$input)), {
      
      # Test reactive dependencies
      session$flushReact()
      
      # Verify outputs
      for (output_name in names(scenario$expected)) {
        expect_equal(
          output[[output_name]], 
          scenario$expected[[output_name]],
          label = paste("Scenario:", scenario$name, 
                        "Output:", output_name)
        )
      }
      
      # Test error handling
      if (!is.null(scenario$should_error)) {
        expect_error(
          output[[scenario$should_error$output]],
          scenario$should_error$pattern
        )
      }
    })
  }
}
```

## Subsection 3: Integration Testing {#integration-testing}

第一項：UI-Server 整合測試 (UI-Server integration testing)

```r
# Test complete component integration
test_component_integration <- function(ui_func, server_func, defaults_func) {
  app <- shinyApp(
    ui = fluidPage(
      ui_func("test", defaults_func())
    ),
    server = function(input, output, session) {
      # Test with various data streams
      test_data <- reactiveVal(create_test_data("normal"))
      
      # Initialize server
      server_func("test", data = test_data)
      
      # Simulate user interactions
      observe({
        # Test UI updates trigger server responses
        session$sendInputMessage("test-filter", 
                                  list(value = "Active"))
      })
      
      # Test server changes update UI
      observe({
        invalidateLater(1000)
        test_data(create_test_data(sample(c("normal", "empty"), 1)))
      })
    }
  )
  
  # Run app and check for errors
  expect_error(app, NA)
}
```

# Section 5: Error Handling and Recovery {#error-handling}

## Subsection 1: Graceful Degradation {#degradation}

```r
# Components should render sensibly with invalid data
graceful_component <- function(id, data = reactive(NULL)) {
  moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {
    
    # Safe data access with fallback
    safe_data <- reactive({
      tryCatch({
        d <- data()
        if (is.null(d)) return(default_empty_data())
        if (!is.data.frame(d)) return(as.data.frame(d))
        if (nrow(d) == 0) return(default_empty_data())
        validate_data_structure(d)
      }, error = function(e) {
        showNotification(
          paste("Data error:", e$message),
          type = "warning"
        )
        default_empty_data()
      })
    })
    
    # Render with error boundaries
    output$plot <- renderPlot({
      tryCatch({
        create_plot(safe_data())
      }, error = function(e) {
        plot_error_message(e$message)
      })
    })
  })
}
```

## Subsection 2: Recovery Testing {#recovery}

```r
# Test component recovery after errors
test_error_recovery <- function(component) {
  testServer(component, {
    # Cause an error
    session$userData$test_data <- reactive(NULL)
    session$flushReact()
    
    # Verify error state
    expect_true(session$userData$in_error_state)
    
    # Provide valid data
    session$userData$test_data <- reactive(create_test_data("normal"))
    session$flushReact()
    
    # Verify recovery
    expect_false(session$userData$in_error_state)
    expect_true(!is.null(output$main_display))
  })
}
```

# Implementation Checklist {#checklist}

:::{.callout-note}
## Component Testing Checklist

**Test Infrastructure:**
- [ ] Standalone test file for each component
- [ ] Test data generators with multiple scenarios
- [ ] Visual test app for manual testing
- [ ] Automated test suite integration

**Test Scenarios:**
- [ ] Normal operation with valid data
- [ ] Empty/NULL data conditions
- [ ] Invalid/malformed data
- [ ] Extreme values and boundaries
- [ ] Performance with large datasets

**Component Aspects:**
- [ ] UI rendering across screen sizes
- [ ] Server logic and calculations
- [ ] Reactive dependencies
- [ ] Error handling and recovery
- [ ] Resource cleanup

**Documentation:**
- [ ] Expected behavior documentation
- [ ] Screenshots of correct states
- [ ] Common failure modes
- [ ] Performance benchmarks

**Quality Metrics:**
- [ ] Test coverage > 80%
- [ ] All edge cases covered
- [ ] Performance within limits
- [ ] Accessibility standards met
:::

# Conclusion {#conclusion}

The Component-wise Testing Principle ensures that complex interactive applications remain reliable and maintainable by testing at the component level. This approach enables faster development, better error isolation, and more comprehensive test coverage. By creating explicit test environments for each component, teams can build more robust applications that handle edge cases gracefully and maintain quality throughout their lifecycle.

---

*Based on: Testing & Deployment Law Article 3*  
*Derives from: [DEV_P001 Debug Principles](../../CH03_development_methodology/principles/DEV_P001_debug_principles.qmd)*  
*Influences: [UI_R001 UI-Server-Defaults N-tuple](../../CH04_ui_components/rules/UI_R001_ui_server_defaults_ntuple.qmd)*  
*Related to: [TD_P002 Dual Testing Approach](TD_P002_dual_testing_approach.qmd), [TD_R001 Shiny Test Data](../rules/TD_R001_shiny_test_data.qmd)*
